biography: Matti made his entry into the challenging environment of the Hector Trophée on two distinct occasions. His participation, though limited in number, contributed to the competitive fabric of this esteemed pairs competition, marking his presence with a quiet resolve on the course.

Across these two appearances, Matti consistently engaged with the demanding format of the multi-day event. While the ultimate prizes, both the coveted Hector Trophée for the winning partnership and the individual Victor Trophy, eluded his grasp, his efforts underscored a genuine commitment to the sport and the unique spirit of the tournament.

Presently, Matti is considered to have concluded his active involvement in Hector events, with the upcoming Hector Trophée 2026 proceeding without his direct participation. His brief yet notable tenure remains an integral part of the tournament's unfolding history, a testament to those who have lent their skill and passion to its legacy, while acknowledging that the possibility of future changes always exists.
